title: Supreme Court Rules Right to Walk on Footpaths is a Fundamental Right
summary: The Supreme Court of India has ruled that the right to walk on footpaths constitutes a fundamental right of citizens. The ruling reinforces the state's obligation to maintain encroachment-free, accessible public walkways. The judgment connects pedestrian access to the right to life and personal liberty under Article 21 of the Constitution. This expands the judicial interpretation of fundamental rights to include safe public mobility.
exam_angle: Directly relevant to the judicial expansion of Article 21 (Right to Life) and the doctrine of positive obligations of the state; a classic example of the Supreme Court reading new rights into Article 21.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Right established: Right to walk on footpaths as a Fundamental Right
Constitutional provision involved: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Significance: Expands the scope of Article 21 to include safe pedestrian access

title: Supreme Court Rules Collegium Decisions Are Outside Judicial Review
summary: The Supreme Court refused to direct the Himachal Pradesh High Court Collegium on a judge's elevation plea, stating that collegium decisions fall outside the scope of judicial review. The court also expressed reluctance to open proceedings of the collegium to scrutiny, calling it a 'Pandora's box.' This reaffirms the opaque but constitutionally entrenched nature of the collegium system for judicial appointments. The ruling reinforces the collegium's supremacy in matters of judicial elevation.
exam_angle: Central to GS Paper 2 questions on judicial appointments, the collegium system, and the debate around judicial accountability and transparency; links to the NJAC judgment (2015) and Articles 124 and 217.
key_facts: Collegium decisions declared outside judicial review by Supreme Court
Case involved: Plea seeking direction to Himachal Pradesh HC Collegium on judge elevation
Constitutional articles relevant: Articles 124 and 217 (appointment of judges)
SC described opening collegium proceedings as opening a 'Pandora's box'

title: India Seeks Preferential Market Access in Trade Deal with the United States
summary: India's Trade Minister Piyush Goyal stated that India is seeking preferential market access as part of ongoing bilateral trade deal negotiations with the United States. The announcement signals India's strategic priorities in securing favourable tariff terms amid evolving US trade policy. This comes in the context of ongoing India-US trade negotiations following the suspension of reciprocal tariffs. Preferential access would benefit key Indian export sectors including textiles, pharmaceuticals, and IT services.
exam_angle: Relevant for India-US bilateral relations, trade policy, and WTO-compatible preferential trade agreement frameworks; important for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy/Trade).
key_facts: Statement by: Trade Minister Piyush Goyal
India's demand: Preferential market access in India-US trade deal
Context: Ongoing India-US bilateral trade negotiations
Key sectors likely affected: Textiles, pharmaceuticals, IT services, agriculture

title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks Continue; Trump Issues Warning Amid Negotiations
summary: US President Donald Trump issued a sharp warning to Iran even as nuclear agreement negotiations continue, stating Iranian negotiators 'won't make it back' if talks fail. Israel's PM Netanyahu is reported to be struggling to formulate a response to a potential US-Iran nuclear framework. The ongoing talks have significant implications for Middle East stability and global non-proliferation efforts. The situation highlights the tension between US diplomatic engagement with Iran and Israel's security concerns.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) on nuclear non-proliferation (NPT, IAEA), Middle East geopolitics, and India's stakes in West Asia (energy imports, diaspora, connectivity projects like IMEC).
key_facts: US-Iran nuclear negotiations ongoing as of May 2025
Trump issued direct warning to Iran amid talks
Israel's Netanyahu reportedly struggling to respond to a potential US-Iran deal
Relevant bodies: IAEA; relevant treaty: NPT (Non-Proliferation Treaty)

title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns — 6th Premature PM Exit in 10 Years
summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has resigned, becoming the sixth British Prime Minister to leave office prematurely within a decade. This reflects ongoing political instability in the United Kingdom following the turbulent post-Brexit political landscape. Starmer had led the Labour Party to a landslide victory in July 2024 before resigning. The development has implications for India-UK bilateral relations, including the ongoing Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations.
exam_angle: Important for 'Persons in News' and GS Paper 2 (IR); also relevant for the India-UK FTA negotiations context and understanding Westminster-style parliamentary democracy.
key_facts: UK PM Keir Starmer resigned
6th UK PM to exit prematurely in 10 years
Starmer led Labour Party to victory in July 2024 general elections
India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) negotiations may be impacted

title: China Positioned as Strategic Winner in Ongoing West Asia Conflict
summary: Analysis indicates that China, despite staying out of the West Asia conflict directly, stands to emerge as one of the biggest strategic beneficiaries of the regional instability. China has been deepening economic and diplomatic ties with Gulf nations and Iran, including through the Belt and Road Initiative (BRI). Reduced US credibility in the region is seen as creating space for Chinese influence. This has direct implications for India's strategic interests in West Asia.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) on China's growing influence in West Asia, India's energy security, and the India-Middle East-Europe Corridor (IMEC) vs. BRI competition.
key_facts: China seen as strategic beneficiary of West Asia conflict without direct involvement
China deepening ties with Gulf states and Iran via BRI
US influence seen declining in the region
Implications for India's energy security and West Asia policy

title: India's Economy Booming but Foreign Institutional Investors Turning Cautious
summary: Despite India's strong macroeconomic growth trajectory, foreign investors have been showing reduced appetite for Indian equities, raising questions about investment sentiment. Key concerns cited include currency volatility, elevated valuations, global risk-off sentiments, and geopolitical uncertainties. The trend of Foreign Portfolio Investor (FPI) outflows has been observed even as domestic institutional investors provide support. This divergence between macroeconomic fundamentals and FPI behaviour is a notable market phenomenon.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy) on capital flows, FPI/FDI dynamics, and factors affecting investment climate; connects to SEBI regulations and RBI's capital account management.
key_facts: India GDP growth remains strong, yet FPI/FII outflows continue
Concerns: currency risk, high valuations, global risk-off sentiment
Domestic Institutional Investors (DIIs) acting as counterbalance
Relevant regulators: SEBI (foreign portfolio investment regulations), RBI

title: KickSky Space Lab: Indian Space Startup Receives Investment from Ideabaaz Platform
summary: Startup discovery platform Ideabaaz has acquired a stake in KickSky Space Lab, an Indian space-tech startup. This investment reflects the growing private space ecosystem in India following the liberalisation of the space sector under the Indian Space Policy 2023 and the establishment of IN-SPACe. KickSky Space Lab is engaged in space technology development, adding to the expanding portfolio of private space startups in India. The deal underscores the role of startup ecosystems in advancing India's space commercialisation agenda.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech/Space) on privatisation of India's space sector, IN-SPACe, and the Indian Space Policy 2023; also relevant for questions on startups and innovation ecosystems.
key_facts: Investor: Ideabaaz (startup discovery platform)
Investee: KickSky Space Lab (Indian space-tech startup)
Regulatory context: Indian Space Policy 2023; IN-SPACe (Indian National Space Promotion and Authorisation Centre)
Significance: Reflects growing private participation in India's space sector

title: Atomberg Creates India-Wide Water Quality Map Accessible via PIN Code
summary: Indian company Atomberg has developed a nationwide map of water quality that allows citizens to check Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) levels in their local water supply using their PIN code. The initiative creates a publicly accessible water quality database at a granular geographic level across India. TDS is a key indicator of water potability and contamination levels. This citizen-facing tool has implications for public health awareness, water governance, and the Jal Jeevan Mission's goals of safe drinking water access.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Environment/Science) on water quality, and GS Paper 2 (Governance) on citizen access to public health data; connects to Jal Jeevan Mission and National Water Policy.
key_facts: Initiative by: Atomberg (Indian technology company)
Feature: India-wide water quality map with PIN-code-based TDS lookup
TDS (Total Dissolved Solids): Key indicator of water potability
Policy connection: Jal Jeevan Mission (safe drinking water to all households)

title: Supreme Court Enhances Compensation to Rs 1.46 Crore for Road Accident Paraplegic Victim
summary: The Supreme Court of India has enhanced compensation to Rs 1.46 crore for a Kerala youth who was rendered paraplegic in a road accident. The ruling reflects judicial activism in ensuring just and adequate compensation for victims of road accidents under the Motor Vehicles Act. The case highlights the court's role in protecting the rights of accident victims, particularly those with permanent disabilities. Such judgments have precedential value for computing compensation under structured formula guidelines set by previous SC rulings.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (Polity/Judiciary) on judicial activism, and GS Paper 3 (Economy) in the context of road safety policy; also links to Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act 2019 and compensation frameworks.
key_facts: Court: Supreme Court of India
Compensation enhanced to: Rs 1.46 crore
Victim: Kerala youth rendered paraplegic in a road accident
Relevant legislation: Motor Vehicles Act; Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act 2019
